name: Monte Canraso Loop from Tudela
description: Jog this moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) circular route from Tudela, gaining 423 feet (129 metres) with views of the town and cereal fields.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Tudela offer a diverse range of experiences, primarily shaped by the Ebro River and its surrounding green corridors. The region features accessible paths along the riverbanks, alongside more varied terrain including local hills and the unique semi-desert landscapes of the nearby Bardenas Reales Natural Park. These natural features provide a mix of paved surfaces and natural trails suitable for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Tudela The most popular jogging route is Ebro Green Corridor, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This route offers diverse scenery along the Ebro River on accessible paths. Another top favourite among local runners is Ebro Green Corridor: Tudela - El Bocal Trail, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. This route is suitable for all skill levels and features mostly paved surfaces, including historical landmarks like the El Bocal Dam. Local runners also love the Monte Canraso Loop from Tudela, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through local hills, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Tudela is defined by the Ebro River, semi-desert landscapes, and local hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging loops. The routes in Tudela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Tudela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Tudela?
A: There are over 70 running routes available around Tudela, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These include easy paths, moderate trails, and some more challenging routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Tudela?
A: Jogging routes around Tudela offer diverse terrain, primarily shaped by the Ebro River and its green corridors, featuring accessible paths along the riverbanks. You'll also find varied landscapes including local hills and the unique semi-desert environment of the nearby Bardenas Reales Natural Park. This mix provides both paved surfaces and natural trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Tudela?
A: The running routes in Tudela are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Tudela's varied terrain, often praising the diverse scenery along the Ebro River and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Tudela?
A: Yes, Tudela offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. For instance, parts of the Ebro Green Corridor provide accessible paths with minimal elevation gain. Overall, there are 5 easy routes available.

Q: Can I find circular or loop running routes in Tudela?
A: Absolutely. Tudela features several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145710832/tour-around-the-perimeter-of-tudela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the perimeter of Tudela — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 10 km. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143768260/monte-canraso-loop-from-tudela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Canraso Loop from Tudela&lt;/a&gt;, leading through local hills.

Q: Are there running paths along the Ebro River?
A: Yes, the Ebro Green Corridor is a prime location for runners, offering accessible paths along the Ebro River. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144302323/ebro-green-corridor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Green Corridor&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145744280/ebro-green-corridor-tudela-el-bocal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Green Corridor: Tudela - El Bocal Trail&lt;/a&gt; provide diverse scenery and mostly paved surfaces, with the latter including historical landmarks like the El Bocal Dam.

Q: Are the running trails around Tudela suitable for families?
A: Many of Tudela's running paths, especially those along the Ebro Green Corridor and within parks like Virgen de la Cabeza, are suitable for families. These routes often feature mostly paved surfaces and offer varying distances, making them accessible for different age groups and fitness levels.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while jogging in Tudela?
A: While jogging in Tudela, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. Along the Ebro Green Corridor, you might pass the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1389734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bocal Dam&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5624960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart of Jesus&lt;/a&gt; monument on Santa Barbara hill, part of Virgen de la Cabeza Park, is a scenic spot. Further afield, the unique 'cabezos' like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt; in Bardenas Reales Natural Park offer a spectacular backdrop for exploration.

Q: Are there any running routes that explore the natural landscapes near Tudela?
A: Yes, beyond the river paths, you can explore routes that venture into the unique natural landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145258863/virgen-de-la-cabeza-park-and-canraso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Cabeza Park and Canraso Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you through local hills. The nearby Bardenas Reales Natural Park, while primarily for hiking, offers a spectacular semi-desert environment with distinctive 'cabezos' that can inspire trail runners seeking a different kind of outdoor experience.

Q: Are there challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Tudela offers 8 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145258863/virgen-de-la-cabeza-park-and-canraso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Cabeza Park and Canraso Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 16 km with significant elevation gain, providing a more demanding workout.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer views of the city or historical sites?
A: Many routes offer glimpses of Tudela's charm. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145710832/tour-around-the-perimeter-of-tudela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the perimeter of Tudela — circular&lt;/a&gt; provides varied perspectives. The Virgen de la Cabeza Park, with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5624960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart of Jesus&lt;/a&gt; monument on Santa Barbara hill, offers panoramic views of the Ribera Navarra. Additionally, the historic Bridge over the Ebro River and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5784182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de los Fueros de Tudela&lt;/a&gt; are picturesque settings within the urban environment.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by lakes or ponds?
A: While the primary focus is the Ebro River, some routes or nearby areas offer access to smaller bodies of water. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortinas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zapata Pond&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the 'Lakes around Tudela' guide, providing a different natural setting for your run or a post-run visit.
tips:
The Ebro Bridge in Tudela is an impressive work of medieval architecture. Built of stone, it is 360 meters long and consists of 16 piers and 17 unequal arches. This bridge is fundamental to the history of Tudela, as it has been a strategic crossing over the Ebro River since its construction in the 12th and 13th centuries.

Although tradition attributes its construction to King Sancho VII the Strong, some historians suggest it could be a reconstruction of an earlier Muslim bridge. Over the years, it has undergone multiple renovations to withstand floods and the passage of time.
They can enjoy landscaped areas, rest areas, walking paths and children's play areas. In addition, there is a small hermitage dedicated to the Virgen de la Cabeza, a fountain and a viewpoint.

The Church of the Company of Mary, also known as the Church of La Enseñanza, is one of the finest examples of Baroque architecture in Tudela. Built between 1732 and 1742, this church stands out for its octagonal design and lantern dome.
Located on Plaza de San Juan and Calle de la Enseñanza, the church is part of a complex that includes a convent and a school, both of which were later renovated.
It seems incredible that this beautiful church can be found a stone's throw from the imposing cathedral.

Tudela is a charming city with much to offer!
Tudela Cathedral: A stunning example of Gothic and Romanesque architecture, with its famous Judgment Gate.
Plaza de los Fueros: The heart of the city, ideal for strolling and soaking up the local atmosphere.
Muñoz Sola Museum of Modern Art: Perfect for art lovers, with a collection of 19th-century works.
Bridge over the Ebro River: A picturesque spot to enjoy views of the river.
Also, don't miss the opportunity to try the delicious local cuisine, especially the fresh vegetables from Tudela's gardens.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Monte Canraso trail?
faqs.answer: The trailhead for this route is conveniently located next to a parking area, making it easy to start your jog.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Canraso route a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Monte Canraso — circular desde Tudela" is a circular route, meaning you'll end up back at your starting point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied surface, including paved tracks, unpaved sections, gravel, and asphalt. You'll traverse between cereal fields, providing a mix of surfaces suitable for jogging.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate for jogging. While it's not overly long (approximately 9.3 km with 129 meters of elevation gain), it does require a good level of physical fitness due to its length and varied terrain. Beginners might find it challenging but achievable with preparation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations aren't provided, considering the agricultural landscape and open views, spring and autumn would likely offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer might be hot due to exposure to the sun.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Canraso trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near roads, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and jog the Monte Canraso — circular desde Tudela trail.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll cross the Bridge over the Ebro River, pass through Parque de la Virgen de la Cabeza, and see religious buildings like the Church of the Compañía de María, Church of San Nicolás, and the Cathedral of Santa María de Tudela. From Monte Canraso itself, you can enjoy views of Tudela and nearby antennas.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a mix of agricultural landscapes, primarily cereal fields, with open views. Upon reaching Monte Canraso, you'll be treated to panoramic views of the town of Tudela and the surrounding area.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections of the Camino Jacobeo del Ebro, Corredor Verde del Ebro, Camino de la Vera Cruz (El camino de los Templarios), and Camino natural de Ebro - Tramo central, among others.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: While the trail starts conveniently next to a parking area, specific public transport options directly to the trailhead are not detailed. It's advisable to check local bus routes in Tudela for connections to the starting point.
